75510.subject: 📰 AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest — July 5
75510.body:
AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-july-5-oleh-bezuhlyi-rvhzf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BFeLD0tfKQymx6%2BKNZ8Q22Q%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0TAthfZCQMwIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mr7ztblx~rl-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mr7ztblx-rl
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: Bitcoin and Ether are clawing back from multi-year lows as ETF buyers return, even as regulators tighten their grip on stablecoins and prediction markets, and Bitcoin's own community squares off over a contentious protocol upgrade. 📈 Bitcoin, Ether Rally as ETF Buyers Return Bitcoin and Ether bounced off multi-year lows as dip buyers stepped back into the market. Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded a $221 million inflow on July 2, signaling renewed institutional appetite after weeks of extreme-fear sentiment. Read full story 🏛️ SEC's Atkins Touts "Historic Steps" Toward On-Chain Markets SEC Chairman Paul Atkins said the agency is accelerating efforts to modernize US financial markets by moving them on-chain. His remarks, delivered at an economic policy event, align with the administration's push to position the US as a leading crypto hub. More details 🏦 BIS: Stablecoins Can't Function Without Central Banks The Bank for International Settlements argued that stablecoins ultimately depend on central bank infrastructure to operate reliably. The report frames that reliance as a risk to monetary sovereignty and calls for tighter, unified oversight of the sector. Read more ⚙️ Bitcoin's BIP-110 Fork Fight Hits an August Deadline A brewing dispute over Bitcoin's proposed BIP-110 upgrade is forcing exchanges, wallets, and mining pools to declare their positions ahead of an August lock-in window. Miner support currently sits at a thin 0.42%, leaving the fork's fate very much unresolved. See details ⚠️ Polymarket Faces Scrutiny Over Deceptive Marketing Polymarket is facing allegations that it used fake trades and paid influencers to inflate its platform's visibility. The claims could invite fresh regulatory attention to prediction markets, a sector already under pressure from EU regulators. Read full story 🇧🇷 Brazil's Central Bank Proposes Stablecoin Transfer Holds Brazil's central bank is proposing mandatory 24-hour holds on large dollar-stablecoin transfers as part of new compliance measures. The rule would primarily affect cross-border transactions and could raise costs for service providers operating in the country. More details 🏢 Securitize Tokenizes $295M in Stock on Solana at IPO Securitize brought $295 million worth of stock onto Solana as part of a fresh IPO, marking one of the network's largest tokenized-equit

75502.subject: Well Informed: How little exercise can you get away with?
75502.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjh9AAcAAAAAA5d2CFEHGYfpS--K_uNhCfG3kHq03oyQRGFNYrRO5bnA6551PcAlDb9TQZpGMchPYEeW2sJEHwKZ6AK9K8Y-Asf4Bo07X0BXxdeCKblZISgy 

July 5th 2026

 

 
 
 
Well Informed

 Your evidence-based guide to health and wellness


 
 
 






 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjh9AAcAAAAAA5d2CFEI5gHGjy8Lp5tPeUu0YQd5IXlJyw2OI3B44fHQpCaFh_B0qCvf4LxwqSzfla8EGVsNFheWKU8KmMqqdudrwT2qOVqNQjSyap5fCVeV 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjh9AAcAAAAAA5d2CFEJJMPzcJ5Q3CeMqX52ir5WaF_Tj032y6bYzrbVkjUoDtUJAyYzCpeXB_3zx7kSBf4KUeV2zoAyATkb_wLJ-t2b3s08RhbGSSvCY8yz 



 How little exercise can you get away with?


 Tim Cross

Senior science writer


 I am reliably informed that there are people out there who enjoy cardio. My officemate claims to like nothing more than spending hours running up and down fells in various windswept bits of the country, and can talk enthusiastically about ideas like “the pain cave”. Although I try to drag myself out for a couple of short runs every week, for me it’s a bit like brushing your teeth: something you do because it’s good for you.


 My favourite sport is bouldering, which involves brief bursts of hard work followed by several minutes spent sitting on the mats chatting to your mates. So imagine my delight when 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjh9AAcAAAA
